◆ printPCRelImm()

void X86ATTInstPrinter::printPCRelImm ( const MCInst * MI,
unsigned OpNo,
raw_ostream & O )

printPCRelImm - This is used to print an immediate value that ends up being encoded as a pc-relative value (e.g. for jumps and calls). These print slightly differently than normal immediates. For example, a $ is not emitted.

Definition at line 141 of file X86ATTInstPrinter.cpp.

References llvm::dyn_cast(), llvm::MCExpr::EvaluateAsAbsolute(), llvm::MCInstPrinter::formatHex(), llvm::MCInstPrinter::formatImm(), llvm::MCOperand::getExpr(), llvm::MCOperand::getImm(), llvm::MCOperand::isExpr(), and llvm::MCOperand::isImm().

Referenced by printInst().
